tracked_binary_files.note: Production DB — migrated to 94924 rows with bet_index. Contains replay evidence. Approaching GitHub 100 MB hard limit.
P188 pre-migration backup. Audit trail. Slightly over 50 MB recommendation.
SHA256 hash file — tiny text file, safe to track in git

option_A.authorization_phrase: YES start P194 create PR from current local commit plan only
option_A.description: Create a feature branch from current HEAD, push the feature branch (no branch protection on feature branches), open a PR to main, let 'replay-default-validation' CI run, merge if CI passes.
risks: Large binary warning (96MB DB, 51MB backup) will still appear on the PR
GitHub may warn about file sizes but will not necessarily block the PR merge
The production DB binary in git history creates long-term repo bloat (every clone downloads 96MB DB)
Future pushes may fail if DB grows past 100 MB hard limit
Other contributors may have slow clones
benefits: Respects branch protection — CI runs before merge
P161-P191 research artifacts, tests, and scripts get published
Quickest path to getting non-binary content published
option_A.recommendation: CONDITIONAL — acceptable only if team explicitly accepts large binary in repo history

option_B.authorization_phrase: YES start P194 remove DB binaries from local commit plan only
option_B.description: Rewrite the P191 local commit to exclude lottery_api/data/lottery_v2.db and backups/p188_*.db. Add these paths to .gitignore. Store the DB and backup outside git (local-only, secure storage, or separate artifact store). Commit only: SHA256 hash file, all research artifacts, tests, scripts, roadmap docs. Then push via PR (Option A path) after binary removal.
steps_required: 6. Verify local DB is still intact (NOT deleted)
7. Create feature branch and push for PR
risks: Requires local history rewrite (git reset) — must be separately authorized
Production DB must NOT be deleted during the process
Backup must NOT be deleted — keep locally outside git
If DB paths are added to .gitignore, future DB state changes stay local-only (requires manual backup/snapshot strategy outside git)
benefits: Eliminates large binary from git history entirely
Repo remains lightweight for all clones
No approaching GitHub 100 MB hard limit
Research artifacts, tests, scripts, roadmap docs all get published cleanly
Best long-term solution for a repo with a growing production DB
option_B.recommendation: PRIMARY RECOMMENDED — safest and cleanest long-term approach
option_B.db_preservation_guarantee: Production DB and backup MUST remain intact on local disk. Removing from git does NOT delete local files. Only removes from git tracking.

option_C.authorization_phrase: YES start P194 git LFS feasibility plan only
option_C.description: Configure Git Large File Storage (LFS) for *.db files. Migrate existing large files in history with git-lfs migrate. Re-push with LFS pointers replacing binary blobs.
risks: Requires git-lfs install and GitHub LFS quota
All contributors must have git-lfs installed for correct checkout
LFS bandwidth/storage costs on GitHub (beyond free tier)
History rewrite via git-lfs migrate affects all commits referencing the large files
Complex setup; not recommended without repo-wide policy decision
benefits: Keeps files 'in git' semantically while avoiding binary blob storage limits
Clean history without blob size issues
option_C.recommendation: NOT RECOMMENDED without explicit repo policy decision and LFS quota budget

option_D.authorization_phrase: NOT AUTHORIZED — branch protection must not be bypassed
option_D.description: Temporarily remove the 'replay-default-validation' required check from main branch protection, direct-push, then re-enable.
risks: Bypasses required CI governance — 'replay-default-validation' exists for a reason
Direct push to main without CI is a governance violation
Large binary problem (96MB DB) is NOT resolved by this option
Requires GitHub admin access
Sets a bad precedent for future governance bypass
option_D.recommendation: EXPLICITLY NOT RECOMMENDED — do not bypass branch protection

option_E.authorization_phrase: YES start P194 keep local commit unpushed and document state
option_E.description: Accept that the P191 local commit stays on local main only. Document the state. The 6-commit local-only chain (P124-P128 + P191) remains unpublished.
risks: Remote repo (origin/main) stays at P124 baseline — no recovery of P128, P191 work on remote
Local-only state is fragile — if local repo is lost, all work since P124 is gone
Other collaborators cannot access P128/P191 changes
benefits: No action required — zero risk of data loss from bad push
option_E.recommendation: ACCEPTABLE as short-term holding state, not as final resolution

primary_rationale: The production DB (lottery_v2.db, 96MB) does not belong in git history. It is a runtime artifact, not source code.
Committing a 96MB binary means every git clone downloads 96MB. This will only grow as more data is ingested.
The DB is approaching GitHub's 100MB hard limit. Future data ingestion will push it over.
The backup DB (51MB) similarly doesn't belong in git — the SHA256 hash is sufficient evidence.
Removing binaries from the commit and adding them to .gitignore is reversible and safe, as long as the local DB file is preserved.
After binary removal, all research artifacts, tests, scripts, drift guard, and roadmap docs can be pushed cleanly via PR (Option B + A combined).
cto_recommendation.secondary: Option A — CREATE_PR_FROM_FEATURE_BRANCH (after Option B binary removal)
cto_recommendation.secondary_rationale: Branch protection requires 'replay-default-validation' CI. A PR from a feature branch is the correct way to satisfy this. After Option B removes binaries, open a PR for the text-only content.
do_not: Do NOT push DB binary blindly (96MB approaching hard limit)
Do NOT delete the local migrated production DB (it is the authoritative state)
Do NOT delete the backup DB locally (needed as rollback evidence)
cto_recommendation.db_governance_note: Removing DB from git tracking does NOT delete the local file. The production DB (lottery_v2.db at 94924 rows, bet_index PRESENT) MUST remain intact on local disk as the authoritative migrated state. Only git tracking is removed.
cto_recommendation.gitignore_strategy: Add to .gitignore: lottery_api/data/lottery_v2.db, lottery_api/data/*.db.bak_*, backups/*.db (except sha256). Store DB migrations and schema changes as SQL files only. Document DB location in README or ops runbook.
